title: IPH 732 - The First Pigeonpea Hybrid for Tamil Nadu, India
abstract: Pigeon pea (Cajanus cajan) hybrid IPH732, developed from a cross between male sterile T21 and ICPL87109, was evaluated in multilocational trials during 1990/91 and in adaptive research trials (ART) during the rainy season of 1992. Overall in multilocational trials, ART, station and national trials, IPH732 had a mean yield of 0.94 t/ha, which was 49 and 41% more than ICPH8 and ICPL87, respectively. IPH732 has an indeterminate growth habit, matures in 115-120 days, and is suitable for irrigated and rainfed conditions. It has good cooking quality and seeds contain 22.3% protein. It was released for cultivation in Tamil Nadu as CoH1.
